1 --- 2 author: plan-writer 3 estimated_complexity: 13 4 --- 5 6 # Parlour Oculus Page 7 8 ## Summary 9 10 Add an Oculus page to the Parlour apparatus that provides a realtime chat UI for consult conversations with animas. The page includes a role selector, codex selector, session sidebar, streaming chat interface, and cost card. Prerequisite changes extend the Loom's API with role listing and enrich the Parlour's `TurnSummary` with session output and token data. 11 12 ## Current State 13 14 **Parlour apparatus** (`packages/plugins/parlour/`): Fully implemented multi-turn conversation management. `ParlourApi` exposes `create`, `takeTurn`, `takeTurnStreaming`, `nextParticipant`, `end`, `list`, `show`. Its `supportKit` contributes two Stacks books (`conversations`, `turns`) and three tools (`conversation-list`, `conversation-show`, `conversation-end`). No `pages` or `routes` are contributed. 15 16 The `show()` method already reads `SessionDoc` records from the Animator's sessions book to aggregate `totalCostUsd`, but does not pull `output` or `tokenUsage` from those records into the returned `TurnSummary`: 17 18 ```typescript 19 // Current TurnSummary — no output, no cost, no tokens 20 interface TurnSummary { 21 sessionId: string | null; 22 turnNumber: number; 23 participant: string; 24 message: string | null; // the prompt sent TO the anima, not the response 25 startedAt: string; 26 endedAt: string | null; 27 } 28 ``` 29 30 **Loom apparatus** (`packages/plugins/loom/`): Provides `LoomApi` with only `weave()`. Role definitions are stored in two places: guild-defined roles in `guild().guildConfig().loom?.roles` (a `Record`) and kit-contributed roles in an internal `kitRoles` Map. There is no public API to enumerate roles. 31 32 **Oculus apparatus** (`packages/plugins/oculus/`): Serves pages contributed via `supportKit.pages` (`PageContribution[]`) and custom routes via `supportKit.routes` (`RouteContribution[]`). Pages are resolved from `path.join(guild.home, 'node_modules', packageName, page.dir)`. Chrome (nav bar + shared CSS) is auto-injected into each page's `index.html`. No real page contributions exist in the codebase yet. 33 34 **Codexes apparatus** (`packages/plugins/codexes/`): Provides `ScriptoriumApi` with `list()`, `show()`, `openDraft()`, etc. The `codex-list` tool is auto-mapped by Oculus to `GET /api/codex/list`. `openDraft()` creates a git worktree and returns a `DraftRecord` with an absolute `path` to the worktree directory. 35 36 **Existing REST routes** (auto-mapped from tools by Oculus): 37 - `GET /api/conversation/list` — lists conversations 38 - `GET /api/conversation/show?id=...` — shows conversation detail 39 - `POST /api/conversation/end` — ends a conversation 40 - `GET /api/codex/list` — lists registered codexes 41 42 ## Requirements 43 44 - R1: The Loom's `LoomApi` interface must include a `listRoles()` method that returns all roles in the system — both guild-defined and kit-contributed — as an array of role info objects. 45 - R2: The Loom must contribute `role-list` and `role-show` tools via its `supportKit.tools`. 46 - R3: The Parlour's `TurnSummary` type must include `output` (the anima's response text), `costUsd`, and `tokenUsage` fields, populated from `SessionDoc` data in `parlour.show()`. 47 - R4: The Parlour must contribute a page to the Oculus with id `'parlour'` and title `'Parlour'`, served from `src/static/parlour/`. 48 - R5: The Parlour must contribute four custom API routes via `supportKit.routes`: `GET /api/parlour/roles`, `GET /api/parlour/conversations`, `POST /api/parlour/create`, and `POST /api/parlour/turn`. 49 - R6: The page must display a role selector dropdown, populated with all system roles sorted alphabetically. 50 - R7: The page must display a codex selector dropdown, populated from the codex-list endpoint, with an empty/none option that defaults to the guild home directory. 51 - R8: When a role is selected, a left-side sidebar must display active consult conversations for that role, sorted by `createdAt` descending. Each entry shows the topic if non-null, otherwise the first human message (truncated), otherwise the formatted `createdAt` date/time. 52 - R9: The sidebar must always be visible when a role is selected. It must include a "New Conversation" action and a way to end each conversation (setting status to `'concluded'`). Ended conversations must not appear in the sidebar. 53 - R10: The chat interface must display conversation turns left-aligned with color differentiation and participant name labels. Human messages and anima messages must use distinct background colors from the Tokyo Night palette. 54 - R11: The message input must be a textarea with 3 initial rows (expandable), a send button, and Ctrl+Enter keyboard shortcut to send. 55 - R12: When the UI is waiting for an anima response, pulsing dots must appear in the chat area and the send button must be disabled. The system must reject attempts to send multiple messages before the anima responds. 56 - R13: `tool_use` and `tool_result` chunks must render as compact inline indicators (pill/badge with tool name), not full details. 57 - R14: `POST /api/parlour/turn` must accept `{ conversationId?: string, role?: string, message: string, codexName?: string }`. When `conversationId` is absent, it must create a new consult conversation (using the codex worktree as `cwd` if `codexName` is provided, otherwise `guild().home`), record the human turn, start the anima turn with streaming, and return the response as SSE. 58 - R15: When a new conversation is created via the turn endpoint, the first SSE event must be a `conversation_created` event containing `{ conversationId, participants }`. 59 - R16: SSE chunks must be emitted for each `ConversationChunk` from `parlour.takeTurnStreaming()`. Text chunks, tool_use chunks, tool_result chunks, and the final `turn_complete` chunk must all be forwarded. 60 - R17: Errors during streaming (animator failure, conversation ended, etc.) must be delivered as an `error` SSE event and displayed inline in the chat as a system message. 61 - R18: A cost card must be displayed in the sidebar, below the session list and aligned to the bottom of the chat UI. It must show total input tokens, total output tokens, and total USD cost, aggregated from per-turn data. It must update after each turn completes. 62 - R19: On initial page load (before any role is selected), the page must show an empty state with the role dropdown and a prompt to select a role. The chat area and sidebar must be hidden or show placeholder text. 63 - R20: When selecting a different role, the chat area must clear and the sidebar must update to show conversations for the new role. No conversation is auto-created. 64 - R21: Selecting an existing conversation from the sidebar must load its full history (including anima response text from the enriched `TurnSummary.output`) and allow continuing the conversation. 65 - R22: The "New Conversation" action must use lazy creation — the conversation record is only created when the user sends the first message, not when "New Conversation" is clicked. 66 - R23: When a codex is selected for a new conversation, the server must call `ScriptoriumApi.openDraft()` to create a worktree and use the draft's `path` as the conversation's `cwd`. The codexes apparatus must be accessed conditionally (it may not be installed). 67 - R24: The page must be implemented as vanilla HTML/CSS/JS with no build step or framework. 68 69 ## Design 70 71 ### Dependencies (Loom Changes) 72 73 The Loom apparatus must be modified to support role enumeration. These changes are a minimum enabling prerequisite. 74 75 **New type — `RoleInfo`:** 76 77 ```typescript 78 interface RoleInfo { 79 /** Role name. Guild-defined roles use plain names; kit roles use qualified names (pluginId.roleName). */ 80 name: string; 81 /** Where this role was defined. */ 82 source: 'guild' | 'kit'; 83 /** Permission grants. */ 84 permissions: string[]; 85 /** Whether permissionless tools are excluded. */ 86 strict?: boolean; 87 } 88 ``` 89 90 **Extended `LoomApi`:** 91 92 ```typescript 93 interface LoomApi { 94 weave(request: WeaveRequest): Promise; 95 96 /** 97 * List all roles known to the system — guild-defined and kit-contributed. 98 * Returns an array of RoleInfo sorted by name. 99 */ 100 listRoles(): RoleInfo[]; 101 } 102 ``` 103 104 The implementation in `createLoom()` iterates `config.roles` (guild-defined, `source: 'guild'`) and `kitRoles` (kit-contributed, `source: 'kit'`), merges them into a sorted array, and returns it synchronously. Guild-defined roles that override kit roles (by qualified name) appear once with `source: 'guild'`. 105 106 **New tools** added to the Loom's `supportKit.tools`: 107 108 `role-list` tool: 109 - `name: 'role-list'` 110 - `description: 'List all roles defined in the guild'` 111 - `permission: 'read'` 112 - `params: {}` (no parameters) 113 - Handler calls `guild().apparatus('loom').listRoles()` and returns the array. 114 115 `role-show` tool: 116 - `name: 'role-show'` 117 - `description: 'Show details of a specific role'` 118 - `permission: 'read'` 119 - `params: { name: z.string() }` 120 - Handler calls `listRoles()`, finds the role by name, returns it. Throws if not found. 121 122 **Files changed:** 123 - `packages/plugins/loom/src/loom.ts` — add `RoleInfo` type, add `listRoles()` to `LoomApi` and `api` object, add tool imports, add tools to `supportKit` 124 - `packages/plugins/loom/src/index.ts` — re-export `RoleInfo` type 125 - `packages/plugins/loom/src/tools/role-list.ts` — new file 126 - `packages/plugins/loom/src/tools/role-show.ts` — new file 127 128 The Loom currently has no `supportKit`. Add one: 129 130 ```typescript 131 supportKit: { 132 tools: [roleList, roleShow], 133 }, 134 ``` 135 136 ### Type Changes 137 138 **`packages/plugins/parlour/src/types.ts` — Extended `TurnSummary`:** 139 140 ```typescript 141 export interface TurnSummary { 142 sessionId: string | null; 143 turnNumber: number; 144 participant: string; 145 message: string | null; 146 startedAt: string; 147 endedAt: string | null; 148 /** The anima's response text. Populated from SessionDoc.output. Null for human turns or when no output was recorded. */ 149 output: string | null; 150 /** Cost in USD for this turn. Null for human turns. */ 151 costUsd: number | null; 152 /** Token usage for this turn. Null for human turns. */ 153 tokenUsage: { 154 inputTokens: number; 155 outputTokens: number; 156 cacheReadTokens?: number; 157 cacheWriteTokens?: number; 158 } | null; 159 } 160 ``` 161 162 **`packages/plugins/loom/src/loom.ts` — New `RoleInfo` type** (see Dependencies section above). 163 164 ### Behavior 165 166 #### `parlour.show()` enrichment 167 168 When `parlour.show()` builds the `turnSummaries` array, it already iterates `convTurns` and fetches `SessionDoc` for each anima turn (to aggregate cost). The enrichment adds three fields to each turn summary: 169 170 - When `t.sessionId` is not null, look up the `SessionDoc` via `sessions.get(t.sessionId)`. 171 - Set `output: session?.output ?? null`. 172 - Set `costUsd: session?.costUsd ?? null`. 173 - Set `tokenUsage: session?.tokenUsage ?? null`. 174 - For human turns (`t.sessionId === null`), set all three to `null`. 175 176 This consolidates the existing session-fetch loop (which currently only aggregates `totalCostUsd`) — each session is fetched once and used for both the aggregate and the per-turn fields. 177 178 #### Custom Route Handlers 179 180 All routes are defined in `packages/plugins/parlour/src/routes.ts` and imported into `parlour.ts` for the `supportKit.routes` array. 181 182 The routes file exports a function that takes the `ParlourApi` reference (or accesses it via `guild()`) and returns `RouteContribution[]` (duck-typed, no oculus import). 183 184 **`GET /api/parlour/roles`:** 185 186 1. Call `guild().apparatus('loom').listRoles()`. 187 2. Return `c.json(roles)` — the array of `RoleInfo` objects. 188 189 **`GET /api/parlour/conversations`:** 190 191 Query params: `role` (required), `status` (optional, default `'active'`). 192 193 1. Call `parlour.list({ status, kind: 'consult', limit: 50 })`. 194 2. Filter results in-memory: keep conversations where `conv.participants.some(p => p.name === role)`. 195 3. For each conversation, determine the display title: 196 - If `conv.topic` is non-null and non-empty, use it. 197 - Otherwise, call `parlour.show(conv.id)` and find the first turn where `participantKind === 'human'` and `message` is non-null. Truncate to 60 characters. 198 - If no human message found, format `conv.createdAt` as a readable date/time string. 199 4. Return `c.json(results)` — each entry includes `id`, `title`, `createdAt`, `turnCount`, `totalCostUsd`. 200 201 Note: The `TurnSummary` field is `participant` (a string name), not `participantKind`. To determine which turns are human, match `participant` against the conversation's participants list where `kind === 'human'`. Alternatively, since `show()` enriches turns with `output`, human turns will have `output === null` and `sessionId === null`. 202 203 **`POST /api/parlour/create`:** 204 205 Body: `{ role: string, codexName?: string }`. 206 207 1. Determine `cwd`: 208 - If `codexName` is provided, attempt `guild().apparatus('codexes')`. If the apparatus is available, call `scriptorium.openDraft({ codexName })` to create a worktree. Use `draft.path` as `cwd`. 209 - If codexes apparatus is not available or `codexName` is not provided, use `guild().home`. 210 2. Call `parlour.create({ kind: 'consult', participants: [{ kind: 'human', name: 'User' }, { kind: 'anima', name: role }], cwd })`. 211 3. Return `c.json({ conversationId, participants })`. 212 213 **`POST /api/parlour/turn`:** 214 215 Body: `{ conversationId?: string, role?: string, message: string, codexName?: string }`. 216 217 This is the SSE streaming endpoint. It uses Hono's `streamSSE` helper from `hono/streaming`. 218 219 1. **Validation:** 220 - If `conversationId` is absent, `role` must be present (400 otherwise). 221 - `message` must be a non-empty string (400 otherwise). 222 223 2. **Conversation resolution:** 224 - If `conversationId` is provided, use it. Look up the conversation via `parlour.show(conversationId)` to get participant IDs. 225 - If `conversationId` is absent, create the conversation: 226 - Determine `cwd` using the same logic as `POST /api/parlour/create` (codex worktree or guild home). 227 - Call `parlour.create(...)`. 228 - Emit SSE event: `event: conversation_created`, `data: JSON.stringify({ conversationId, participants })`. 229 230 3. **Identify participants** from the conversation: find the participant with `kind === 'human'` (the human) and `kind === 'anima'` (the anima). 231 232 4. **Human turn:** Call `parlour.takeTurn({ conversationId, participantId: humanParticipant.id, message })`. 233 234 5. **Anima turn (streaming):** Call `parlour.takeTurnStreaming({ conversationId, participantId: animaParticipant.id })`. 235 236 6. **Stream chunks:** Iterate `chunks` from the streaming handle. For each chunk: 237 - Emit SSE event: `event: chunk`, `data: JSON.stringify(chunk)`. 238 - The `turn_complete` chunk (with `costUsd`) is forwarded as-is. 239 240 7. **Error handling:** If any step throws, emit SSE event: `event: error`, `data: JSON.stringify({ error: message })`. Then close the stream. 241 242 8. **Completion:** After all chunks are yielded, await the `result` promise to ensure turn recording completes, then close the stream. 243 244 #### `supportKit` Changes in `parlour.ts` 245 246 The parlour's `supportKit` is extended with `pages` and `routes`: 247 248 ```typescript 249 supportKit: { 250 books: { 251 conversations: { indexes: ['status', 'kind', 'createdAt'] }, 252 turns: { indexes: ['conversationId', 'turnNumber', 'participantId', 'participantKind'] }, 253 }, 254 tools: [conversationList, conversationShow, conversationEnd], 255 pages: [ 256 { id: 'parlour', title: 'Parlour', dir: 'src/static/parlour' }, 257 ], 258 routes: parlourRoutes, // imported from './routes.ts' 259 }, 260 ``` 261 262 The `routes` value is an array of `{ method, path, handler }` objects. No oculus types are imported — the Oculus duck-types the supportKit via `as OculusKit`. 263 264 #### `package.json` Changes 265 266 In `packages/plugins/parlour/package.json`: 267 268 - Add `"hono": "^4.7.11"` to `dependencies` (needed for `Context` type in route handlers and `streamSSE` import). 269 - Change `"files"` from `["dist"]` to `["dist", "src/static"]`. 270 271 #### Static Page (`src/static/parlour/`) 272 273 Three files: 274 275 **`index.html`** — page shell: 276 277 ```html 278 279 280 281 282 283 Parlour 284 285 286 287
288
289 290 291
292
293
294 295
296
297

Cost

298
299

Select or start a conversation
303
304 305 306
307
308
309
310 311 312 313 ``` 314 315 The Oculus chrome injection adds the shared `style.css` link and nav bar automatically. The page's own `parlour.css` provides page-specific styles. 316 317 **`parlour.css`** — page-specific styles using the Oculus CSS custom properties: 318 319 Layout: 320 - `#parlour-app` fills the viewport below the nav. 321 - `#parlour-toolbar` is a horizontal bar with the role and codex dropdowns. 322 - `#parlour-main` is a flex row: sidebar (fixed width ~260px) + chat area (flex-grow). 323 - `#parlour-sidebar` is a flex column: "New Conversation" button at top, scrollable conversation list in middle, cost card pinned to bottom. 324 - `#parlour-chat` is a flex column: scrollable `#chat-messages` (flex-grow) + `#chat-input-area` (fixed at bottom). 325 326 Message styling: 327 - `.message` — base class for all messages, left-aligned, with padding and border-radius. 328 - `.message--human` — background: `var(--surface2)`, left border: `3px solid var(--blue)`. 329 - `.message--anima` — background: `var(--surface)`, left border: `3px solid var(--green)`. 330 - `.message--system` — background: transparent, color: `var(--text-dim)`, italic, centered. 331 - `.message-author` — small label above the message text, color `var(--text-dim)`. 332 - `.tool-indicator` — inline pill/badge: `background: var(--surface2)`, `color: var(--text-dim)`, `font-size: 11px`, `border-radius: 4px`, `padding: 2px 8px`. 333 334 Loading indicator: 335 - `.typing-indicator` — three dots that pulse using the existing `@keyframes pulse` animation from the Oculus CSS. 336 337 Cost card: 338 - Uses the existing `.card` class. 339 - Token counts displayed as `IN: 12,345 OUT: 6,789`. 340 - USD displayed as `$0.42`. 341 342 Sidebar conversations: 343 - `.conversation-item` — clickable row with title, hover highlight. 344 - `.conversation-item--active` — background: `var(--surface2)` to indicate selected. 345 - `.conversation-item .end-btn` — small button (appears on hover) to end the conversation. 346 347 Hidden utility: 348 - `.hidden { display: none; }` — used to hide `#parlour-main` before role selection and `#cost-card` before any turn. 349 350 **`app.js`** — vanilla JS application logic: 351 352 State: 353 ```javascript 354 let currentRole = null; 355 let currentCodex = ''; // empty string = guild home 356 let currentConversationId = null; 357 let isStreaming = false; 358 ``` 359 360 Functions (key behaviors): 361 362 `loadRoles()` — On page load, fetch `GET /api/parlour/roles`. --- author: plan-writer estimated_complexity: 13 --- # Parlour Oculus Page ## Summary Add an Oculus page to the Parlour apparatus that provides a realtime chat UI for consult conversations with animas. The page includes a role selector, codex selector, session sidebar, streaming chat interface, and cost card. Prerequisite changes extend the Loom's API with role listing and enrich the Parlour's `TurnSummary` with session output and token data. ## Current State **Parlour apparatus** (`packages/plugins/parlour/`): Fully implemented multi-turn conversation management. `ParlourApi` exposes `create`, `takeTurn`, `takeTurnStreaming`, `nextParticipant`, `end`, `list`, `show`. Its `supportKit` contributes two Stacks books (`conversations`, `turns`) and three tools (`conversation-list`, `conversation-show`, `conversation-end`). No `pages` or `routes` are contributed. The `show()` method already reads `SessionDoc` records from the Animator's sessions book to aggregate `totalCostUsd`, but does not pull `output` or `tokenUsage` from those records into the returned `TurnSummary`: ```typescript // Current TurnSummary — no output, no cost, no tokens interface TurnSummary { sessionId: string | null; turnNumber: number; participant: string; message: string | null; // the prompt sent TO the anima, not the response startedAt: string; endedAt: string | null; } ``` **Loom apparatus** (`packages/plugins/loom/`): Provides `LoomApi` with only `weave()`. Role definitions are stored in two places: guild-defined roles in `guild().guildConfig().loom?.roles` (a `Record`) and kit-contributed roles in an internal `kitRoles` Map. There is no public API to enumerate roles. **Oculus apparatus** (`packages/plugins/oculus/`): Serves pages contributed via `supportKit.pages` (`PageContribution[]`) and custom routes via `supportKit.routes` (`RouteContribution[]`). Pages are resolved from `path.join(guild.home, 'node_modules', packageName, page.dir)`. Chrome (nav bar + shared CSS) is auto-injected into each page's `index.html`. No real page contributions exist in the codebase yet. **Codexes apparatus** (`packages/plugins/codexes/`): Provides `ScriptoriumApi` with `list()`, `show()`, `openDraft()`, etc. The `codex-list` tool is auto-mapped by Oculus to `GET /api/codex/list`. `openDraft()` creates a git worktree and returns a `DraftRecord` with an absolute `path` to the worktree directory. **Existing REST routes** (auto-mapped from tools by Oculus): - `GET /api/conversation/list` — lists conversations - `GET /api/conversation/show?id=...` — shows conversation detail - `POST /api/conversation/end` — ends a conversation - `GET /api/codex/list` — lists registered codexes ## Requirements - R1: The Loom's `LoomApi` interface must include a `listRoles()` method that returns all roles in the system — both guild-defined and kit-contributed — as an array of role info objects. - R2: The Loom must contribute `role-list` and `role-show` tools via its `supportKit.tools`. - R3: The Parlour's `TurnSummary` type must include `output` (the anima's response text), `costUsd`, and `tokenUsage` fields, populated from `SessionDoc` data in `parlour.show()`. - R4: The Parlour must contribute a page to the Oculus with id `'parlour'` and title `'Parlour'`, served from `src/static/parlour/`. - R5: The Parlour must contribute four custom API routes via `supportKit.routes`: `GET /api/parlour/roles`, `GET /api/parlour/conversations`, `POST /api/parlour/create`, and `POST /api/parlour/turn`. - R6: The page must display a role selector dropdown, populated with all system roles sorted alphabetically. - R7: The page must display a codex selector dropdown, populated from the codex-list endpoint, with an empty/none option that defaults to the guild home directory. - R8: When a role is selected, a left-side sidebar must display active consult conversations for that role, sorted by `createdAt` descending. Each entry shows the topic if non-null, otherwise the first human message (truncated), otherwise the formatted `createdAt` date/time. - R9: The sidebar must always be visible when a role is selected. It must include a "New Conversation" action and a way to end each conversation (setting status to `'concluded'`). Ended conversations must not appear in the sidebar. - R10: The chat interface must display conversation turns left-aligned with color differentiation and participant name labels. Human messages and anima messages must use distinct background colors from the Tokyo Night palette. - R11: The message input must be a textarea with 3 initial rows (expandable), a send button, and Ctrl+Enter keyboard shortcut to send. - R12: When the UI is waiting for an anima response, pulsing dots must appear in the chat area and the send button must be disabled. The system must reject attempts to send multiple messages before the anima responds. - R13: `tool_use` and `tool_result` chunks must render as compact inline indicators (pill/badge with tool name), not full details. - R14: `POST /api/parlour/turn` must accept `{ conversationId?: string, role?: string, message: string, codexName?: string }`. When `conversationId` is absent, it must create a new consult conversation (using the codex worktree as `cwd` if `codexName` is provided, otherwise `guild().home`), record the human turn, start the anima turn with streaming, and return the response as SSE. - R15: When a new conversation is created via the turn endpoint, the first SSE event must be a `conversation_created` event containing `{ conversationId, participants }`. - R16: SSE chunks must be emitted for each `ConversationChunk` from `parlour.takeTurnStreaming()`. Text chunks, tool_use chunks, tool_result chunks, and the final `turn_complete` chunk must all be forwarded. - R17: Errors during streaming (animator failure, conversation ended, etc.) must be delivered as an `error` SSE event and displayed inline in the chat as a system message. - R18: A cost card must be displayed in the sidebar, below the session list and aligned to the bottom of the chat UI. It must show total input tokens, total output tokens, and total USD cost, aggregated from per-turn data. It must update after each turn completes. - R19: On initial page load (before any role is selected), the page must show an empty state with the role dropdown and a prompt to select a role. The chat area and sidebar must be hidden or show placeholder text. - R20: When selecting a different role, the chat area must clear and the sidebar must update to show conversations for the new role. No conversation is auto-created. - R21: Selecting an existing conversation from the sidebar must load its full history (including anima response text from the enriched `TurnSummary.output`) and allow continuing the conversation. - R22: The "New Conversation" action must use lazy creation — the conversation record is only created when the user sends the first message, not when "New Conversation" is clicked. - R23: When a codex is selected for a new conversation, the server must call `ScriptoriumApi.openDraft()` to create a worktree and use the draft's `path` as the conversation's `cwd`. The codexes apparatus must be accessed conditionally (it may not be installed). - R24: The page must be implemented as vanilla HTML/CSS/JS with no build step or framework. ## Design ### Dependencies (Loom Changes) The Loom apparatus must be modified to support role enumeration. These changes are a minimum enabling prerequisite. **New type — `RoleInfo`:** ```typescript interface RoleInfo { /** Role name. Guild-defined roles use plain names; kit roles use qualified names (pluginId.roleName). */ name: string; /** Where this role was defined. */ source: 'guild' | 'kit'; /** Permission grants. */ permissions: string[]; /** Whether permissionless tools are excluded. */ strict?: boolean; } ``` **Extended `LoomApi`:** ```typescript interface LoomApi { weave(request: WeaveRequest): Promise; /** * List all roles known to the system — guild-defined and kit-contributed. * Returns an array of RoleInfo sorted by name. */ listRoles(): RoleInfo[]; } ``` The implementation in `createLoom()` iterates `config.roles` (guild-defined, `source: 'guild'`) and `kitRoles` (kit-contributed, `source: 'kit'`), merges them into a sorted array, and returns it synchronously. Guild-defined roles that override kit roles (by qualified name) appear once with `source: 'guild'`. **New tools** added to the Loom's `supportKit.tools`: `role-list` tool: - `name: 'role-list'` - `description: 'List all roles defined in the guild'` - `permission: 'read'` - `params: {}` (no parameters) - Handler calls `guild().apparatus('loom').listRoles()` and returns the array. `role-show` tool: - `name: 'role-show'` - `description: 'Show details of a specific role'` - `permission: 'read'` - `params: { name: z.string() }` - Handler calls `listRoles()`, finds the role by name, returns it. Throws if not found. **Files changed:** - `packages/plugins/loom/src/loom.ts` — add `RoleInfo` type, add `listRoles()` to `LoomApi` and `api` object, add tool imports, add tools to `supportKit` - `packages/plugins/loom/src/index.ts` — re-export `RoleInfo` type - `packages/plugins/loom/src/tools/role-list.ts` — new file - `packages/plugins/loom/src/tools/role-show.ts` — new file The Loom currently has no `supportKit`. Add one: ```typescript supportKit: { tools: [roleList, roleShow], }, ``` ### Type Changes **`packages/plugins/parlour/src/types.ts` — Extended `TurnSummary`:** ```typescript export interface TurnSummary { sessionId: string | null; turnNumber: number; participant: string; message: string | null; startedAt: string; endedAt: string | null; /** The anima's response text. Populated from SessionDoc.output. Null for human turns or when no output was recorded. */ output: string | null; /** Cost in USD for this turn. Null for human turns. */ costUsd: number | null; /** Token usage for this turn. Null for human turns. */ tokenUsage: { inputTokens: number; outputTokens: number; cacheReadTokens?: number; cacheWriteTokens?: number; } | null; } ``` **`packages/plugins/loom/src/loom.ts` — New `RoleInfo` type** (see Dependencies section above). ### Behavior #### `parlour.show()` enrichment When `parlour.show()` builds the `turnSummaries` array, it already iterates `convTurns` and fetches `SessionDoc` for each anima turn (to aggregate cost). The enrichment adds three fields to each turn summary: - When `t.sessionId` is not null, look up the `SessionDoc` via `sessions.get(t.sessionId)`. - Set `output: session?.output ?? null`. - Set `costUsd: session?.costUsd ?? null`. - Set `tokenUsage: session?.tokenUsage ?? null`. - For human turns (`t.sessionId === null`), set all three to `null`. This consolidates the existing session-fetch loop (which currently only aggregates `totalCostUsd`) — each session is fetched once and used for both the aggregate and the per-turn fields. #### Custom Route Handlers All routes are defined in `packages/plugins/parlour/src/routes.ts` and imported into `parlour.ts` for the `supportKit.routes` array. The routes file exports a function that takes the `ParlourApi` reference (or accesses it via `guild()`) and returns `RouteContribution[]` (duck-typed, no oculus import). **`GET /api/parlour/roles`:** 1. Call `guild().apparatus('loom').listRoles()`. 2. Return `c.json(roles)` — the array of `RoleInfo` objects. **`GET /api/parlour/conversations`:** Query params: `role` (required), `status` (optional, default `'active'`). 1. Call `parlour.list({ status, kind: 'consult', limit: 50 })`. 2. Filter results in-memory: keep conversations where `conv.participants.some(p => p.name === role)`. 3. For each conversation, determine the display title: - If `conv.topic` is non-null and non-empty, use it. - Otherwise, call `parlour.show(conv.id)` and find the first turn where `participantKind === 'human'` and `message` is non-null. Truncate to 60 characters. - If no human message found, format `conv.createdAt` as a readable date/time string. 4. Return `c.json(results)` — each entry includes `id`, `title`, `createdAt`, `turnCount`, `totalCostUsd`. Note: The `TurnSummary` field is `participant` (a string name), not `participantKind`. To determine which turns are human, match `participant` against the conversation's participants list where `kind === 'human'`. Alternatively, since `show()` enriches turns with `output`, human turns will have `output === null` and `sessionId === null`. **`POST /api/parlour/create`:** Body: `{ role: string, codexName?: string }`. 1. Determine `cwd`: - If `codexName` is provided, attempt `guild().apparatus('codexes')`. If the apparatus is available, call `scriptorium.openDraft({ codexName })` to create a worktree. Use `draft.path` as `cwd`. - If codexes apparatus is not available or `codexName` is not provided, use `guild().home`. 2. Call `parlour.create({ kind: 'consult', participants: [{ kind: 'human', name: 'User' }, { kind: 'anima', name: role }], cwd })`. 3. Return `c.json({ conversationId, participants })`. **`POST /api/parlour/turn`:** Body: `{ conversationId?: string, role?: string, message: string, codexName?: string }`. This is the SSE streaming endpoint. It uses Hono's `streamSSE` helper from `hono/streaming`. 1. **Validation:** - If `conversationId` is absent, `role` must be present (400 otherwise). - `message` must be a non-empty string (400 otherwise). 2. **Conversation resolution:** - If `conversationId` is provided, use it. Look up the conversation via `parlour.show(conversationId)` to get participant IDs. - If `conversationId` is absent, create the conversation: - Determine `cwd` using the same logic as `POST /api/parlour/create` (codex worktree or guild home). - Call `parlour.create(...)`. - Emit SSE event: `event: conversation_created`, `data: JSON.stringify({ conversationId, participants })`. 3. **Identify participants** from the conversation: find the participant with `kind === 'human'` (the human) and `kind === 'anima'` (the anima). 4. **Human turn:** Call `parlour.takeTurn({ conversationId, participantId: humanParticipant.id, message })`. 5. **Anima turn (streaming):** Call `parlour.takeTurnStreaming({ conversationId, participantId: animaParticipant.id })`. 6. **Stream chunks:** Iterate `chunks` from the streaming handle. For each chunk: - Emit SSE event: `event: chunk`, `data: JSON.stringify(chunk)`. - The `turn_complete` chunk (with `costUsd`) is forwarded as-is. 7. **Error handling:** If any step throws, emit SSE event: `event: error`, `data: JSON.stringify({ error: message })`. Then close the stream. 8. **Completion:** After all chunks are yielded, await the `result` promise to ensure turn recording completes, then close the stream. #### `supportKit` Changes in `parlour.ts` The parlour's `supportKit` is extended with `pages` and `routes`: ```typescript supportKit: { books: { conversations: { indexes: ['status', 'kind', 'createdAt'] }, turns: { indexes: ['conversationId', 'turnNumber', 'participantId', 'participantKind'] }, }, tools: [conversationList, conversationShow, conversationEnd], pages: [ { id: 'parlour', title: 'Parlour', dir: 'src/static/parlour' }, ], routes: parlourRoutes, // imported from './routes.ts' }, ``` The `routes` value is an array of `{ method, path, handler }` objects. No oculus types are imported — the Oculus duck-types the supportKit via `as OculusKit`. #### `package.json` Changes In `packages/plugins/parlour/package.json`: - Add `"hono": "^4.7.11"` to `dependencies` (needed for `Context` type in route handlers and `streamSSE` import). - Change `"files"` from `["dist"]` to `["dist", "src/static"]`. #### Static Page (`src/static/parlour/`) Three files: **`index.html`** — page shell: ```html Parlour

Cost

Select or start a conversation
``` The Oculus chrome injection adds the shared `style.css` link and nav bar automatically. The page's own `parlour.css` provides page-specific styles. **`parlour.css`** — page-specific styles using the Oculus CSS custom properties: Layout: - `#parlour-app` fills the viewport below the nav. - `#parlour-toolbar` is a horizontal bar with the role and codex dropdowns. - `#parlour-main` is a flex row: sidebar (fixed width ~260px) + chat area (flex-grow). - `#parlour-sidebar` is a flex column: "New Conversation" button at top, scrollable conversation list in middle, cost card pinned to bottom. - `#parlour-chat` is a flex column: scrollable `#chat-messages` (flex-grow) + `#chat-input-area` (fixed at bottom). Message styling: - `.message` — base class for all messages, left-aligned, with padding and border-radius. - `.message--human` — background: `var(--surface2)`, left border: `3px solid var(--blue)`. - `.message--anima` — background: `var(--surface)`, left border: `3px solid var(--green)`. - `.message--system` — background: transparent, color: `var(--text-dim)`, italic, centered. - `.message-author` — small label above the message text, color `var(--text-dim)`. - `.tool-indicator` — inline pill/badge: `background: var(--surface2)`, `color: var(--text-dim)`, `font-size: 11px`, `border-radius: 4px`, `padding: 2px 8px`. Loading indicator: - `.typing-indicator` — three dots that pulse using the existing `@keyframes pulse` animation from the Oculus CSS. Cost card: - Uses the existing `.card` class. - Token counts displayed as `IN: 12,345 OUT: 6,789`. - USD displayed as `$0.42`. Sidebar conversations: - `.conversation-item` — clickable row with title, hover highlight. - `.conversation-item--active` — background: `var(--surface2)` to indicate selected. - `.conversation-item .end-btn` — small button (appears on hover) to end the conversation. Hidden utility: - `.hidden { display: none; }` — used to hide `#parlour-main` before role selection and `#cost-card` before any turn. **`app.js`** — vanilla JS application logic: State: ```javascript let currentRole = null; let currentCodex = ''; // empty string = guild home let currentConversationId = null; let isStreaming = false; ``` Functions (key behaviors): `loadRoles()` — On page load, fetch `GET /api/parlour/roles`. Populate `#role-select` with `

content.content:
agent-setting
permission-mode
file-history-snapshot
---

You're right that it's reviewer-related, but not in the way you might expect. The reviewer isn't *causing* the contamination — **it's masking it**.

### The mechanism:

1. **Implement session** starts with `cwd: draft.path` (correct worktree). But the agent can see `/workspace/nexus/` in the filesystem. It discovers it (probably via `find`, `ls /workspace`, or following the git remote), decides that's the "real" project, and commits there instead.

2. **Review engine** runs mechanical checks (`pnpm build`, `pnpm test`) in the worktree — which has no commits from implement (they went to the wrong place). It computes the git diff — which is empty. The reviewer sees no work done and issues a **FAIL**.

3. **Revise session** gets the "everything's missing" findings and **redoes all the work from scratch**, this time (usually) staying in the worktree. The commission succeeds.

4. **The contamination commit sits quietly in `/workspace/nexus/`**, diverging the local branch from origin/main.

### Why this is getting worse:

Before the implement→review→revise pipeline existed, a contaminated implement session would simply fail — no commits in the worktree = no seal = visible failure. You'd catch it immediately.

**The review/revise loop creates an invisible recovery path.** The commission succeeds, the work is correct, but the garbage commit accumulates silently in `/workspace/nexus/`. You only notice when the next person (or agent) touches the repo and finds a divergence.

### Three incidents today:
| Commission | Phase that contaminated | Masking? |
|---|---|---|
| `w-mnonwi2y` (Block Checker) | Revise | Yes — revise's Haiku subagent |
| `w-mnowtmnp` (Kit Templates) | Implement | Yes — recovered via revise |
| `w-mnoyb1f0` (Spider Oculus) | Implement | Yes — recovered via revise |

### The real fix:

Container isolation (already in your todo at `.scratch/todo/session-directory-isolation.md`). The agent literally cannot see `/workspace/nexus/` if it doesn't exist in its filesystem. No behavioral guardrail, no soft flags — hard spatial boundary.

### Short-term option:

Want me to add a post-session check to the seal engine? After each session completes, check whether any commits landed in `/workspace/nexus/` that shouldn't be there, and alert immediately rather than letting them accumulate silently. That way the masking effect goes away — you'd see the contamination even when the commission succeeds.
Good instinct on both counts. Let me check for path leaks first.
🚨 Jackpot. There's a lot here. Let me focus on what actually gets into agent prompts:
Now let me check whether the recent Oculus page specs (the ones causing contamination) have this issue, and importantly — what's in the **writ body** that actually gets fed to the agent:
The spec.md itself is clean, but the planner transcripts reference `/workspace`. Those transcripts aren't in the writ body though. Let me check how the commission body is built — what actually lands in the writ:
There it is — the nexus `package.json` has a `vibe` script pointing to `/workspace/vibers`. That's in the repo itself, so it's in every worktree checkout too. An agent exploring the project might see that and follow it to `/workspace/vibers`, and from there `ls /workspace/` reveals `nexus/`.

OK, here's the full picture, Sean:

## Path Leak Audit

**Active contamination vectors** (present in worktree checkouts):
1. **`/workspace/nexus/package.json`** — `"vibe"` script references `/workspace/vibers`. Any agent exploring the project root sees this and can follow the trail to `/workspace/`.

**Inactive but notable** (old specs, won't cause future issues):
2. **8 spec.md files** with absolute `/workspace/nexus/...` paths — these are all for completed commissions, so no future risk. But they were likely a contributing factor in earlier contamination incidents.
3. **8 inventory.md files** — 150 total `/workspace` references (same completed commissions).

**Operational tooling** (not seen by agents):
4. `bin/commission.sh`, `bin/agent-all.sh`, etc. — hardcoded `/workspace/` paths. These don't leak into agent prompts.
5. `guild.json` `sanctumHome` — used by Laboratory, not exposed to agents.

## The `/tmp` Worktree Idea

I like it. The current path is `$GUILD_HOME/.nexus/worktrees/<codex>/<draft-id>`, which means:
- The agent starts in `/workspace/vibers/.nexus/worktrees/nexus/draft-xxx`
- Running `ls ../../../..` gets you to `/workspace/vibers/`
- Running `ls /workspace/` reveals `nexus/`, `nexus-mk2/`, `vibers/`

Moving worktrees to `/tmp` helps two ways:
1. **Less interesting filesystem** — `/tmp` is boring, no sibling directories that look like "the real project"

You're right that it's reviewer-related, but not in the way you might expect. The reviewer isn't *causing* the contamination — **it's masking it**.

### The mechanism:

1. **Implement session** starts with `cwd: draft.path` (correct worktree). But the agent can see `/workspace/nexus/` in the filesystem. It discovers it (probably via `find`, `ls /workspace`, or following the git remote), decides that's the "real" project, and commits there instead.

2. **Review engine** runs mechanical checks (`pnpm build`, `pnpm test`) in the worktree — which has no commits from implement (they went to the wrong place). It computes the git diff — which is empty. The reviewer sees no work done and issues a **FAIL**.

3. **Revise session** gets the "everything's missing" findings and **redoes all the work from scratch**, this time (usually) staying in the worktree. The commission succeeds.

4. **The contamination commit sits quietly in `/workspace/nexus/`**, diverging the local branch from origin/main.

### Why this is getting worse:

Before the implement→review→revise pipeline existed, a contaminated implement session would simply fail — no commits in the worktree = no seal = visible failure. You'd catch it immediately.

**The review/revise loop creates an invisible recovery path.** The commission succeeds, the work is correct, but the garbage commit accumulates silently in `/workspace/nexus/`. You only notice when the next person (or agent) touches the repo and finds a divergence.

### Three incidents today:
| Commission | Phase that contaminated | Masking? |
|---|---|---|
| `w-mnonwi2y` (Block Checker) | Revise | Yes — revise's Haiku subagent |
| `w-mnowtmnp` (Kit Templates) | Implement | Yes — recovered via revise |
| `w-mnoyb1f0` (Spider Oculus) | Implement | Yes — recovered via revise |

### The real fix:

Container isolation (already in your todo at `.scratch/todo/session-directory-isolation.md`). The agent literally cannot see `/workspace/nexus/` if it doesn't exist in its filesystem. No behavioral guardrail, no soft flags — hard spatial boundary.

### Short-term option:

Want me to add a post-session check to the seal engine? After each session completes, check whether any commits landed in `/workspace/nexus/` that shouldn't be there, and alert immediately rather than letting them accumulate silently. That way the masking effect goes away — you'd see the contamination even when the commission succeeds.
Good instinct on both counts. Let me check for path leaks first.

content.content:
- **Provenance tracking** — both registries use the same pattern (private `provenance` map, threaded `pluginId`). Minimal, correct.
- **Topological sort** in the pipeline visualization is correct — handles DAGs with branching, shows upstream IDs for multi-upstream engines.
- **Lazy config loading** — Config tab only fetches on first activation.
- **Type exports** — both `EngineDesignInfo` and `BlockTypeInfo` re-exported from index.ts.

**Nits (livable):**
- **Pipeline arrows are always linear.** The topo sort is correct, but arrows always go between adjacent nodes in the sorted order (idx > 0 → arrow). In a branching DAG, this could show an arrow between two unrelated parallel branches. The spec explicitly says "a horizontal row with listed upstream IDs in each node is acceptable," so this is within spec — but worth noting.
- **`pollIntervalMs` conditional spread** — `...(blockType.pollIntervalMs !== undefined ? { pollIntervalMs: blockType.pollIntervalMs } : {})` works but is noisier than `if (x !== undefined) obj.pollIntervalMs = x`. Cosmetic.

### Tests

- **Spider tests:** 334 passing (0 failures), including new oculus-specific tests in `spider-oculus.test.ts` (212 lines) covering both tools, the config route, and block type listing.
- **Fabricator tests:** 17 passing (0 failures), including new `listEngineDesigns()` tests — happy path, hasCollect, empty registry, duplicate ID.
- **Typecheck:** Clean on both packages.

### Cost Efficiency

$5.50 total across 3 sessions at complexity 13. The review session was $0.20 (91 seconds) — suggests the implement session was clean enough that review had minimal work. Good signal.

### Comparison Note

This is the first plugin-contributed Oculus page, Sean. The chrome injection, page registration, route contribution, and tool auto-mapping patterns all worked end-to-end on the first commission to use them. That's a good validation of the Oculus architecture — the plugin seams held.

---

Want me to run the instrument suite on this one, or shall I record the outcome and move on?


The commission log already has `outcome: success` and `spec_quality_post: strong` recorded. I just need to add `reviewed_at` and `revision_required`.
Now add the coco-log entry and commit.
Done. Recorded as **success, no revision required**, with the review note and `reviewed_at: 2026-04-07`. 🐒
There it is — `0f40d79` is the Clerk Oculus page commit. Let me check it and see what the rig/writ state actually looks like.
There's the issue. The third session (`ses-mnp0f6yv`, the revise session) is stuck in `status: running` with no `ended_at`. The implement and review sessions completed fine, and the commit (`0f40d79`) already landed on main — so the work is done, but the revise session never got its completion recorded by the Laboratory.

This is the same pattern as branch contamination — the session likely finished but the Laboratory's CDC never saw the terminal event, so the rig is still "active."
So here's the picture, Sean:

1. **The work landed.** Commit `0f40d79` is on `main` with the correct author email (`w-mnp02of0-6cfaeeeb8dd0@nexus.local`). 968 lines — writ-types tool, Oculus page, writs dashboard, 137 new test lines.
2. **The revise session is orphaned.** `ses-mnp0f6yv` shows `status: running`, no `ended_at`, no `cost_usd`. The Laboratory never recorded its completion.
3. **The rig/writ are stuck active** because the system thinks the revise engine is still running.

This is likely another contamination-adjacent issue — the revise session committed to `/workspace/nexus/` directly (which is how the commit ended up on `main`), but the seal engine never ran because the rig thinks it's still mid-revise.

**Options:**

1. **Manual cleanup** — Use the Spider tools to mark the revise engine completed and let the rig finish its lifecycle (seal would need to be skipped since the work is already on main).
2. **Just close it out** — Mark the writ as completed directly via the Clerk, accept that the seal step was bypassed this time. Record what happened.

What do you want to do?
Yep — PID `288578` on pts/1, started at 19:27. That's a session provider process (`--print - --output-format stream-json`) running right now. It's the revise session for w-mnp02of0.

Though I see you've already patched the session record and commission log to mark it completed. Is that process hung? Want to kill it, or let it finish?
The `7f62acb5` session is the active one (latest, still being written to). Let me check what it's doing.
Ha! OK, so here's the full story:

1. **The revise session started** and its Explore subagent tried `/root/monorepo/` first (permission denied), then went searching across the filesystem.
2. **It found `/workspace/nexus/`** — the shared clone, not its worktree — and read the code there.
3. **It discovered the work was already done** (commit `0f40d79` already existed on the branch it was reading).
4. **It ran the tests** (105 passing), confirmed everything was in place, tried to commit but found nothing to commit.
5. **It concluded correctly**: "All commission requirements have been fully implemented in commit `0f40d79`."

So the reviser *didn't* reimplement — it was smart enough to see the work was already there and just validated it. But it's **reading `/workspace/nexus/` instead of its worktree**, which is the same contamination pattern we've seen before. The process is still alive (PID 288578) and appears to be at the end of its output — it's probably just sitting there having produced its final summary.

The good news: no double-implementation damage. The bad news: another instance of the worktree escape. Container isolation can't come soon enough. 🐒

Want me to kill the process, or let it die on its own?
OK so the Explore subagent was well-behaved — it read from the correct worktree path. But then when the *parent* session got the subagent's summary back, it hallucinated paths:
😂 There it is. The Explore subagent came back with a *summary* (no paths, no file contents), and the parent session decided it needed to read the files itself. But instead of using the worktree path that's in its own `cwd` (`/workspace/vibers/.nexus/worktrees/nexus/draft-mnp02pi4-b165aff4`), it pulled `/root/monorepo/` out of thin air. Pure hallucination — that path doesn't exist anywhere in the system prompt, the spec, or the subagent's response.

After getting `EACCES`, it went on a `find` expedition across the entire filesystem, eventually stumbled onto `/workspace/nexus/`, and ran with that for the rest of the session.

So the contamination chain was:
1. Subagent correctly reads worktree ✅
2. Subagent returns a prose summary (no paths) ✅
3. Parent session hallucinates `/root/monorepo/` ❌
4. Gets permission denied, panics, runs `find /` 🤦
5. Discovers `/workspace/nexus/` (Sean's clone), reads code there instead
6. Finds work already committed (from the implement session's contamination), declares victory

Two layers of escape — the implement session committed to the wrong place, and then the revise session independently navigated there too. The subagent pattern actually makes this *worse* here, because the summary threw away the correct paths and the parent had to guess.

Let me resolve these conflicts by incorporating both. The rebase is already in progress — let me work through the conflicted files.

Since `d80315f` (local contamination) and `7587ac0` (remote sealed) are both from the same Spider Oculus page commission, the cleanest approach is to **skip the local commit** (the remote is the canonical sealed version) and then cherry-pick just the unique refinements from the local version as a separate small commit.

Let me abort and take that approach.
OK so `d80315f` is the only local-only commit (contamination). The remote has `7587ac0` (same work, sealed) plus `8da4e87` (Loom Oculus page). I'll save the unique refinements from the local commit, then reset to origin/main.

OK Sean, here's the full picture:

## The Conflict

**What happened:** Third contamination incident today. Commission `w-mnoyb1f0` (Spider Oculus Page, cx 13) committed its work to `/workspace/nexus/` at 18:37 (implement session) instead of the draft worktree. The sealed version went to remote at 18:51 (via the revise session). Local had diverged 1 vs 2 from origin.

**Resolution:** Reset to origin/main, cherry-picked the one unique improvement from the contamination commit (added `instructions` fields on `block-types` and `engine-designs` tools). Pushed clean.

## Type Errors

The 5 type errors in the tools package (`failedPlugins` missing on mock Guild, `pluginId` missing on mock resolved tools) are **not new** — they've been latent since commission `w-mnkcbbz1` (Isolate Plugin Failures) added `failedPlugins()` to the Guild interface without updating the tools package's test mocks. They became visible when `abde39a` (kit-contributed rig templates, `w-mnowtmnp`) triggered a rebuild cascade. These will need a quick fixup commission or inline fix.

## Root Cause Analysis — The Contamination Pattern

You're right that it's reviewer-related, but not in the way you might expect. The reviewer isn't *causing* the contamination — **it's masking it**.

### The mechanism:

1. **Implement session** starts with `cwd: draft.path` (correct worktree). But the agent can see `/workspace/nexus/` in the filesystem. It discovers it (probably via `find`, `ls /workspace`, or following the git remote), decides that's the "real" project, and commits there instead.

2. **Review engine** runs mechanical checks (`pnpm build`, `pnpm test`) in the worktree — which has no commits from implement (they went to the wrong place). It computes the git diff — which is empty. The reviewer sees no work done and issues a **FAIL**.

3. **Revise session** gets the "everything's missing" findings and **redoes all the work from scratch**, this time (usually) staying in the worktree. The commission succeeds.

4. **The contamination commit sits quietly in `/workspace/nexus/`**, diverging the local branch from origin/main.

### Why this is getting worse:

Before the implement→review→revise pipeline existed, a contaminated implement session would simply fail — no commits in the worktree = no seal = visible failure. You'd catch it immediately.

**The review/revise loop creates an invisible recovery path.** The commission succeeds, the work is correct, but the garbage commit accumulates silently in `/workspace/nexus/`. You only notice when the next person (or agent) touches the repo and finds a divergence.

### Three incidents today:
| Commission | Phase that contaminated | Masking? |
|---|---|---|
| `w-mnonwi2y` (Block Checker) | Revise | Yes — revise's Haiku subagent |
| `w-mnowtmnp` (Kit Templates) | Implement | Yes — recovered via revise |
| `w-mnoyb1f0` (Spider Oculus) | Implement | Yes — recovered via revise |

### The real fix:

Container isolation (already in your todo at `.scratch/todo/session-directory-isolation.md`). The agent literally cannot see `/workspace/nexus/` if it doesn't exist in its filesystem. No behavioral guardrail, no soft flags — hard spatial boundary.

commit d173575ab3d5f656c02c2be084273cb467559700
Author: Artificer <w-mnoznabx-3f83f1ad2c50@nexus.local>
Date:   Tue Apr 7 19:19:46 2026 +0000

    Add Parlour Oculus page with realtime chat UI and extend Loom with role listing
    
    Loom changes:
    - Add RoleInfo type and listRoles() method to LoomApi, returning all guild-defined
      and kit-contributed roles sorted alphabetically by name
    - Add role-list and role-show tools contributed via supportKit.tools
    - Export RoleInfo from index.ts
    
    Parlour changes:
    - Extend TurnSummary with output, costUsd, and tokenUsage fields populated from
      SessionDoc data in show(); human turns get null for all three
    - Add routes.ts with four custom API routes (GET /api/parlour/roles,
      GET /api/parlour/conversations, POST /api/parlour/create, POST /api/parlour/turn)
      using SSE streaming for the turn endpoint
    - Add Parlour Oculus page (src/static/parlour/) as vanilla HTML/CSS/JS with role
      selector, codex selector, session sidebar with cost card, streaming chat UI,
      typing indicator, and tool_use/tool_result pill indicators
    - Extend supportKit with pages and routes contributions
    - Add hono dependency for route handlers; update files to include src/static
    
    Tests:
    - 8 new listRoles() tests in loom.test.ts covering guild roles, kit roles,
      combined sorted output, guild override, strict flag, and empty state
    - 13 new tests in parlour.test.ts covering show() enrichment (output/costUsd/
      tokenUsage per turn), conversation list filtering logic, and supportKit
      page/route registration

commit b951d951406232f1a12678e58640c25720e6b72e
Author: Artificer <w-mnoznabx-3f83f1ad2c50@nexus.local>
Date:   Tue Apr 7 19:35:52 2026 +0000

    Fix R14 validation, variable shadowing, and add route-handler tests
    
    Three issues identified in review are addressed:
    
    1. Fix variable shadowing: Rename `message` in the catch block of
       `turnRoute()` to `errMessage` to eliminate the inner `const message`
       declaration that shadowed the outer destructured `message` from the
       request body.
    
    2. Fix R14 — HTTP 400 validation: Move body parsing and input validation
       before the `streamSSE()` call in POST /api/parlour/turn so that
       missing role/conversationId or empty message returns a proper HTTP 400
       response rather than a 200 with an SSE error event.
    
    3. Add route-handler integration tests (spec test cases 9-16):
       - GET /api/parlour/roles returns sorted array of role info
       - GET /api/parlour/conversations filters by role
       - GET /api/parlour/conversations excludes non-active conversations
       - POST /api/parlour/turn lazy creation (SSE conversation_created event)
       - POST /api/parlour/turn continues existing conversation (no conversation_created)
       - POST /api/parlour/turn returns HTTP 400 without role/conversationId
       - POST /api/parlour/turn returns HTTP 400 for empty message
       - POST /api/parlour/turn with codexName calls openDraft
       - POST /api/parlour/turn without codexes falls back to guild home
    
       Supporting changes: setup() accepts extraApparatuses to inject mock
       apparatus implementations, registers parlour in the apparatus map
       so route handlers can access it via guild().apparatus('parlour'),
       and adds loom roles to guildConfig() so the roles test has data.
    
    All 57 tests pass.

 packages/plugins/parlour/src/routes.ts       |  58 +++---

Author: Artificer <w-mnoznabx-3f83f1ad2c50@nexus.local>

    Fix R14 validation, variable shadowing, and add route-handler tests
    
    Three issues identified in review are addressed:
    
    1. Fix variable shadowing: Rename `message` in the catch block of
       `turnRoute()` to `errMessage` to eliminate the inner `const message`
       declaration that shadowed the outer destructured `message` from the
       request body.
    
    2. Fix R14 — HTTP 400 validation: Move body parsing and input validation
       before the `streamSSE()` call in POST /api/parlour/turn so that
       missing role/conversationId or empty message returns a proper HTTP 400
       response rather than a 200 with an SSE error event.
    
    3. Add route-handler integration tests (spec test cases 9-16):
       - GET /api/parlour/roles returns sorted array of role info
       - GET /api/parlour/conversations filters by role
       - GET /api/parlour/conversations excludes non-active conversations
       - POST /api/parlour/turn lazy creation (SSE conversation_created event)
       - POST /api/parlour/turn continues existing conversation (no conversation_created)
       - POST /api/parlour/turn returns HTTP 400 without role/conversationId
       - POST /api/parlour/turn returns HTTP 400 for empty message
       - POST /api/parlour/turn with codexName calls openDraft
       - POST /api/parlour/turn without codexes falls back to guild home
    
       Supporting changes: setup() accepts extraApparatuses to inject mock
       apparatus implementations, registers parlour in the apparatus map
       so route handlers can access it via guild().apparatus('parlour'),
       and adds loom roles to guildConfig() so the roles test has data.
    
    All 57 tests pass.

content.text:
Clean build, clean tests. So the commission produced complete, working code that passed all checks — but the rig never sealed it. The `abandoned` outcome was set manually (in the commission log) after the rig got stuck.

Here's the full picture, Sean:

---

## `w-mnoznabx-3f83f1ad2c50` — Parlour Oculus Page

**What happened:**
- 3 sessions ran — implement, review, revise — all exited cleanly (exit code 0)
- Total cost: **$5.67** across ~32 minutes of execution
- The implement session produced `d173575` (1,929 lines — the full feature)
- The review session found 3 issues (variable shadowing, validation ordering, missing route tests)
- The revise session produced `b951d95` (fixes + 9 route handler tests)
- **Both commits landed correctly in the worktree** at `/tmp/nexus-drafts/nexus/draft-mnoznb72-490d2874`
- **All 140 tests pass**, build is clean

**Why it failed:** The rig never advanced to the seal engine. The commission log says `outcome: abandoned`, `failure_mode: execution_error` — but the work is complete and sitting in the worktree, never pushed. Something in the rig lifecycle broke between the revise session completing and the seal engine firing. Likely the same class of rig-stuck issue we saw with `w-mnp02of0` (the Clerk page) where the Laboratory lost track of the session completion event.

**Contamination in `/workspace/nexus/`:** **None from this commission.** The local clone is in sync with `origin/main`. The 640 untracked files are all build artifacts (`.d.ts`, `.js`, `.js.map`) — not from this commission specifically, but from *some* agent running `pnpm build` in the shared clone instead of a worktree. They're harmless but messy.

**The work is recoverable.** Two commits on branch `draft-mnoznb72-490d2874`, ready to merge. We could:

1. **Push it manually** — merge the worktree branch to main, push, mark the commission as success
2. **Re-commission** — waste another $5+ to redo work that's already done and tested
3. **Kick the rig** — if the guild is still running, try to unstick the seal engine

On the build artifacts in `/workspace/nexus/` — yeah, that's an agent running `pnpm build` in the shared clone. Probably leaking from a worktree escape. A `git clean -fdx` on `/workspace/nexus/` would nuke them, but we should make sure nothing depends on them first. Want me to check?

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Commission Log — Standing Research Instrument
#
# One entry per patron-sourced commission. Patron-subjective judgments only.
# Full artifacts live in experiments/data/commissions/<id>/ — see README there.
#
# IDs are writ IDs (w-*) from the Clerk. Pre-Clerk commissions used session
# IDs (ses-*) or ad-hoc labels — these are grandfathered with a note field.
#
# Fields:
#   id                — writ ID (canonical) or legacy session/label ID
#   title             — short description of the commissioned work
#   codex             — target codex name
#   complexity        — patron estimate (Fibonacci: 1 2 3 5 8 13 21)
#   spec_quality_pre  — patron assessment of spec before dispatch
#   outcome           — patron assessment after review
#   revision_required — whether follow-up work was needed
#   spec_quality_post — patron assessment of spec after review
#   reviewed_at       — ISO date when patron reviewed this commission (null = not reviewed)
#                       Backfill note: all commissions through w-mnjp1ph3 were reviewed by
#                       the patron (with Coco) on the dates shown. Dates are approximate,
#                       derived from review-related commit timestamps. After w-mnjp1ph3,
#                       patron review became ad hoc — the planning workshop and static
#                       implement→review→revise rig structure reduced perceived need.
#   failure_mode      — if outcome != success, what went wrong (omit if n/a)
#   note              — meta info about the record itself (not content)
#
# Scales:
#   Complexity (Fibonacci): 1 2 3 5 8 13 21
#   Spec quality: strong | adequate | weak
#   Outcome: success | partial | wrong | abandoned
#   Failure mode: spec_ambiguous | requirement_wrong | execution_error | complexity_overrun | broken | incomplete

No real page contributions exist in the codebase yet.\\n\\n**Codexes apparatus** (`packages/plugins/codexes/`): Provides `ScriptoriumApi` with `list()`, `show()`, `openDraft()`, etc. The `codex-list` tool is auto-mapped by Oculus to `GET /api/codex/list`. `openDraft()` creates a git worktree and returns a `DraftRecord` with an absolute `path` to the worktree directory.\\n\\n**Existing REST routes** (auto-mapped from tools by Oculus):\\n- `GET /api/conversation/list` — lists conversations\\n- `GET /api/conversation/show?id=...` — shows conversation detail\\n- `POST /api/conversation/end` — ends a conversation\\n- `GET /api/codex/list` — lists registered codexes\\n\\n## Requirements\\n\\n- R1: The Loom's `LoomApi` interface must include a `listRoles()` method that returns all roles in the system — both guild-defined and kit-contributed — as an array of role info objects.\\n- R2: The Loom must contribute `role-list` and `role-show` tools via its `supportKit.tools`.\\n- R3: The Parlour's `TurnSummary` type must include `output` (the anima's response text), `costUsd`, and `tokenUsage` fields, populated from `SessionDoc` data in `parlour.show()`.\\n- R4: The Parlour must contribute a page to the Oculus with id `'parlour'` and title `'Parlour'`, served from `src/static/parlour/`.\\n- R5: The Parlour must contribute four custom API routes via `supportKit.routes`: `GET /api/parlour/roles`, `GET /api/parlour/conversations`, `POST /api/parlour/create`, and `POST /api/parlour/turn`.\\n- R6: The page must display a role selector dropdown, populated with all system roles sorted alphabetically.\\n- R7: The page must display a codex selector dropdown, populated from the codex-list endpoint, with an empty/none option that defaults to the guild home directory.\\n- R8: When a role is selected, a left-side sidebar must display active consult conversations for that role, sorted by `createdAt` descending. Each entry shows the topic if non-null, otherwise the first human message (truncated), otherwise the formatted `createdAt` date/time.\\n- R9: The sidebar must always be visible when a role is selected. It must include a \\\"New Conversation\\\" action and a way to end each conversation (setting status to `'concluded'`). Ended conversations must not appear in the sidebar.\\n- R10: The chat interface must display conversation turns left-aligned with color differentiation and participant name labels. Human messages and anima messages must use distinct background colors from the Tokyo Night palette.\\n- R11: The message input must be a textarea with 3 initial rows (expandable), a send button, and Ctrl+Enter keyboard shortcut to send.\\n- R12: When the UI is waiting for an anima response, pulsing dots must appear in the chat area and the send button must be disabled. The system must reject attempts to send multiple messages before the anima responds.\\n- R13: `tool_use` and `tool_result` chunks must render as compact inline indicators (pill/badge with tool name), not full details.\\n- R14: `POST /api/parlour/turn` must accept `{ conversationId?: string, role?: string, message: string, codexName?: string }`. It must show total input tokens, total output tokens, and total USD cost, aggregated from per-turn data. It must update after each turn completes.\\n- R19: On initial page load (before any role is selected), the page must show an empty state with the role dropdown and a prompt to select a role. The chat area and sidebar must be hidden or show placeholder text.\\n- R20: When selecting a different role, the chat area must clear and the sidebar must update to show conversations for the new role. No conversation is auto-created.\\n- R21: Selecting an existing conversation from the sidebar must load its full history (including anima response text from the enriched `TurnSummary.output`) and allow continuing the conversation.\\n- R22: The \\\"New Conversation\\\" action must use lazy creation — the conversation record is only created when the user sends the first message, not when \\\"New Conversation\\\" is clicked.\\n- R23: When a codex is selected for a new conversation, the server must call `ScriptoriumApi.openDraft()` to create a worktree and use the draft's `path` as the conversation's `cwd`. These changes are a minimum enabling prerequisite.\\n\\n**New type — `RoleInfo`:**\\n\\n```typescript\\ninterface RoleInfo {\\n /** Role name. Guild-defined roles use plain names; kit roles use qualified names (pluginId.roleName). */\\n name: string;\\n /** Where this role was defined. */\\n source: 'guild' | 'kit';\\n /** Permission grants. */\\n permissions: string[];\\n /** Whether permissionless tools are excluded. */\\n strict?: boolean;\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Extended `LoomApi`:**\\n\\n```typescript\\ninterface LoomApi {\\n weave(request: WeaveRequest): Promise;\\n\\n /**\\n * List all roles known to the system — guild-defined and kit-contributed.\\n * Returns an array of RoleInfo sorted by name.\\n */\\n listRoles(): RoleInfo[];\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe implementation in `createLoom()` iterates `config.roles` (guild-defined, `source: 'guild'`) and `kitRoles` (kit-contributed, `source: 'kit'`), merges them into a sorted array, and returns it synchronously. Guild-defined roles that override kit roles (by qualified name) appear once with `source: 'guild'`.\\n\\n**New tools** added to the Loom's `supportKit.tools`:\\n\\n`role-list` tool:\\n- `name: 'role-list'`\\n- `description: 'List all roles defined in the guild'`\\n- `permission: 'read'`\\n- `params: {}` (no parameters)\\n- Handler calls `guild().apparatus('loom').listRoles()` and returns the array.\\n\\n`role-show` tool:\\n- `name: 'role-show'`\\n- `description: 'Show details of a specific role'`\\n- `permission: 'read'`\\n- `params: { name: z.string() }`\\n- Handler calls `listRoles()`, finds the role by name, returns it. Throws if not found.\\n\\n**Files changed:**\\n- `packages/plugins/loom/src/loom.ts` — add `RoleInfo` type, add `listRoles()` to `LoomApi` and `api` object, add tool imports, add tools to `supportKit`\\n- `packages/plugins/loom/src/index.ts` — re-export `RoleInfo` type\\n- `packages/plugins/loom/src/tools/role-list.ts` — new file\\n- `packages/plugins/loom/src/tools/role-show.ts` — new file\\n\\nThe Loom currently has no `supportKit`. Add one:\\n\\n```typescript\\nsupportKit: {\\n tools: [roleList, roleShow],\\n},\\n```\\n\\n### Type Changes\\n\\n**`packages/plugins/parlour/src/types.ts` — Extended `TurnSummary`:**\\n\\n```typescript\\nexport interface TurnSummary {\\n sessionId: string | null;\\n turnNumber: number;\\n participant: string;\\n message: string | null;\\n startedAt: string;\\n endedAt: string | null;\\n /** The anima's response text. Populated from SessionDoc.output. Null for human turns or when no output was recorded. */\\n output: string | null;\\n /** Cost in USD for this turn. Null for human turns. */\\n costUsd: number | null;\\n /** Token usage for this turn. Null for human turns. */\\n tokenUsage: {\\n inputTokens: number;\\n outputTokens: number;\\n cacheReadTokens?: number;\\n cacheWriteTokens?: number;\\n } | null;\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**`packages/plugins/loom/src/loom.ts` — New `RoleInfo` type** (see Dependencies section above).\\n\\n### Behavior\\n\\n#### `parlour.show()` enrichment\\n\\nWhen `parlour.show()` builds the `turnSummaries` array, it already iterates `convTurns` and fetches `SessionDoc` for each anima turn (to aggregate cost). The enrichment adds three fields to each turn summary:\\n\\n- When `t.sessionId` is not null, look up the `SessionDoc` via `sessions.get(t.sessionId)`.\\n- Set `output: session?.output ?? null`.\\n- Set `costUsd: session?.costUsd ?? null`.\\n- Set `tokenUsage: session?.tokenUsage ?? null`.\\n- For human turns (`t.sessionId === null`), set all three to `null`.\\n\\nThis consolidates the existing session-fetch loop (which currently only aggregates `totalCostUsd`) — each session is fetched once and used for both the aggregate and the per-turn fields.\\n\\n#### Custom Route Handlers\\n\\nAll routes are defined in `packages/plugins/parlour/src/routes.ts` and imported into `parlour.ts` for the `supportKit.routes` array.\\n\\nThe routes file exports a function that takes the `ParlourApi` reference (or accesses it via `guild()`) and returns `RouteContribution[]` (duck-typed, no oculus import).\\n\\n**`GET /api/parlour/roles`:**\\n\\n1. Call `guild().apparatus('loom').listRoles()`.\\n2. Return `c.json(roles)` — the array of `RoleInfo` objects.\\n\\n**`GET /api/parlour/conversations`:**\\n\\nQuery params: `role` (required), `status` (optional, default `'active'`).\\n\\n1. Call `parlour.list({ status, kind: 'consult', limit: 50 })`.\\n2. Filter results in-memory: keep conversations where `conv.participants.some(p => p.name === role)`.\\n3. For each conversation, determine the display title:\\n - If `conv.topic` is non-null and non-empty, use it.\\n - Otherwise, call `parlour.show(conv.id)` and find the first turn where `participantKind === 'human'` and `message` is non-null. Truncate to 60 characters.\\n - If no human message found, format `conv.createdAt` as a readable date/time string.\\n4. Return `c.json(results)` — each entry includes `id`, `title`, `createdAt`, `turnCount`, `totalCostUsd`.\\n\\nNote: The `TurnSummary` field is `participant` (a string name), not `participantKind`. To determine which turns are human, match `participant` against the conversation's participants list where `kind === 'human'`. Alternatively, since `show()` enriches turns with `output`, human turns will have `output === null` and `sessionId === null`.\\n\\n**`POST /api/parlour/create`:**\\n\\nBody: `{ role: string, codexName?: string }`.\\n\\n1. Determine `cwd`:\\n - If `codexName` is provided, attempt `guild().apparatus('codexes')`. If the apparatus is available, call `scriptorium.openDraft({ codexName })` to create a worktree. Use `draft.path` as `cwd`.\\n - If codexes apparatus is not available or `codexName` is not provided, use `guild().home`.\\n2. Call `parlour.create({ kind: 'consult', participants: [{ kind: 'human', name: 'User' }, { kind: 'anima', name: role }], cwd })`.\\n3. Return `c.json({ conversationId, participants })`.\\n\\n**`POST /api/parlour/turn`:**\\n\\nBody: `{ conversationId?: string, role?: string, message: string, codexName?: string }`.\\n\\nThis is the SSE streaming endpoint. For each chunk:\\n - Emit SSE event: `event: chunk`, `data: JSON.stringify(chunk)`.\\n - The `turn_complete` chunk (with `costUsd`) is forwarded as-is.\\n\\n7. **Error handling:** If any step throws, emit SSE event: `event: error`, `data: JSON.stringify({ error: message })`. Then close the stream.\\n\\n8. **Completion:** After all chunks are yielded, await the `result` promise to ensure turn recording completes, then close the stream.\\n\\n#### `supportKit` Changes in `parlour.ts`\\n\\nThe parlour's `supportKit` is extended with `pages` and `routes`:\\n\\n```typescript\\nsupportKit: {\\n books: {\\n conversations: { indexes: ['status', 'kind', 'createdAt'] },\\n turns: { indexes: ['conversationId', 'turnNumber', 'participantId', 'participantKind'] },\\n },\\n tools: [conversationList, conversationShow, conversationEnd],\\n pages: [\\n { id: 'parlour', title: 'Parlour', dir: 'src/static/parlour' },\\n ],\\n routes: parlourRoutes, // imported from './routes.ts'\\n},\\n```\\n\\nThe `routes` value is an array of `{ method, path, handler }` objects. No oculus types are imported — the Oculus duck-types the supportKit via `as OculusKit`.\\n\\n#### `package.json` Changes\\n\\nIn `packages/plugins/parlour/package.json`:\\n\\n- Add `\\\"hono\\\": \\\"^4.7.11\\\"` to `dependencies` (needed for `Context` type in route handlers and `streamSSE` import).\\n- Change `\\\"files\\\"` from `[\\\"dist\\\"]` to `[\\\"dist\\\", \\\"src/static\\\"]`.\\n\\n#### Static Page (`src/static/parlour/`)\\n\\nThree files:\\n\\n**`index.html`** — page shell:\\n\\n```html\\n\\n\\n\\n \\n \\n Parlour\\n \\n\\n\\n

The page's own `parlour.css` provides page-specific styles.\\n\\n**`parlour.css`** — page-specific styles using the Oculus CSS custom properties:\\n\\nLayout:\\n- `#parlour-app` fills the viewport below the nav.\\n- `#parlour-toolbar` is a horizontal bar with the role and codex dropdowns.\\n- `#parlour-main` is a flex row: sidebar (fixed width ~260px) + chat area (flex-grow).\\n- `#parlour-sidebar` is a flex column: \\\"New Conversation\\\" button at top, scrollable conversation list in middle, cost card pinned to bottom.\\n- `#parlour-chat` is a flex column: scrollable `#chat-messages` (flex-grow) + `#chat-input-area` (fixed at bottom).\\n\\nMessage styling:\\n- `.message` — base class for all messages, left-aligned, with padding and border-radius.\\n- `.message--human` — background: `var(--surface2)`, left border: `3px solid var(--blue)`.\\n- `.message--anima` — background: `var(--surface)`, left border: `3px solid var(--green)`.\\n- `.message--system` — background: transparent, color: `var(--text-dim)`, italic, centered.\\n- `.message-author` — small label above the message text, color `var(--text-dim)`.\\n- `.tool-indicator` — inline pill/badge: `background: var(--surface2)`, `color: var(--text-dim)`, `font-size: 11px`, `border-radius: 4px`, `padding: 2px 8px`.\\n\\nLoading indicator:\\n- `.typing-indicator` — three dots that pulse using the existing `@keyframes pulse` animation from the Oculus CSS.\\n\\nCost card:\\n- Uses the existing `.card` class.\\n- Token counts displayed as `IN: 12,345 OUT: 6,789`.\\n- USD displayed as `$0.42`.\\n\\nSidebar conversations:\\n- `.conversation-item` — clickable row with title, hover highlight.\\n- `.conversation-item--active` — background: `var(--surface2)` to indicate selected.\\n- `.conversation-item .end-btn` — small button (appears on hover) to end the conversation.\\n\\nHidden utility:\\n- `.hidden { display: none; **There it is!** The seal engine tried to rebase the worktree branch onto main and hit conflicts — the same Loom overlap I manually resolved during the cherry-pick. The Loom page commission (`w-mnoznn5z`) had already landed `8da4e87` on main with its own `listRoles()` implementation, so when the Parlour commission's seal tried to rebase, it got conflicts and gave up.

Now let me update the writ and rig status to `completed`.
